name: Italian Anise Cookies (Biscotti D'anici)
recipeIngredient: Zest of 1 medium Orange, finely grated
Zest of 1 medium Lemon, finely grated
2 cups Almonds, coarsely chopped and toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
2. In a large bowl, beat together butter, sugar, salt, orange zest, lemon zest, and anise seed until light.
3. Add eggs one at a time, blending each addition well.
4. Stir in flour, baking powder, and baking soda, then mix in the almonds.
5. Divide dough into thirds.
6. Shape each portion into a log, about 12 inches long and 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
7. Place logs on well-greased baking sheets (two logs per sheet; place the third log on a second sheet).
8. Flatten loaves to about 1-inch thickness using buttered fingers.
9. Bake for 25 minutes, rotating sheets midway through baking.
10. Remove loaves from oven and reduce temperature to 275°F (135°C). Using a thin-bladed knife at a 45-degree angle, cut logs into 3/4-inch slices.
11. Lay the slices, cut-side down, about 1 1/2 inches apart on the baking sheets.
12. Bake for an additional 40 minutes, or until very dry and firm to the touch.
13. Cool cookies on a rack before storing in an airtight container for up to 10 days.
Chocolate-Dipped Biscotti:
- Melt 6 ounces of semisweet chocolate.
- Line two large baking sheets with waxed paper.
- Dip about 3/4 inch of both ends of the cooled cookies into melted chocolate, then place them on the prepared sheets to set.
